publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and no code task for Modules and imports; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Modules and imports: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Python: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Import merely pastes definitions and never executes module code, so circular imports cannot expose incomplete state.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Import executes a module once into a cached module object; keep top-level effects minimal and redesign cycles that depend on partially initialized names.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Import executes a module once into a cached module object; keep top-level effects minimal and redesign cycles that depend on partially initialized names.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.
